picks.thesis: Tech-enabled health insurer taking share from legacy carriers. Q1 EPS beat 85%; medical loss ratio improved 75.4% to 70.5%. P/S ~0.5x vs peers 0.7-1.1x.
picks.falsification: MLR rises above 75% in Q2, OR membership growth turns negative YoY, OR ACA enhanced premium tax credits cut without offset.

picks.thesis: Event-driven: Q1 earnings May 13 BMO. Consensus $316.9M revenue. The AI infra 'first crack' confirmation test after CRWV's miss. Bull case: revenue beat + margin improvement = thesis intact.
picks.falsification: Revenue misses $316.9M consensus, OR margin compression (EBIT% worsens vs Q4), OR forward ARR guidance reduced.
